issue: forceReasoning: true is set unconditionally for gpt-5.x with provider=openai, routing requests through OpenAI Responses API (/v1/responses) which router.tangle.tools and most OpenAI-compatible proxies don't implement
action: Gate the forceReasoning block on absence of this.baseUrl. When baseUrl is set, strip forceReasoning + maxCompletionTokens so the AI SDK uses /v1/chat/completions (the lowest-common-denominator endpoint).
verification: Unit test in tests/brain.test.ts asserting generationOptions returns no providerOptions.openai.forceReasoning when baseUrl is set; re-run npm run validate:landing -- --quick against router and expect non-zero pass rate.

issue: createForceNonStreamingFetch is gated on this.baseUrl with no explicit predicate; the same gate is duplicated for forceReasoning. Diverging conditions are how the next router-specific bug ships silently.
action: Extract isProxiedOpenAI(providerName) helper that returns provider==='openai' && Boolean(this.baseUrl). Use it in both gates as the single source of truth for 'downshift to LCD behavior because we're talking to a proxy'.
verification: Unit test asserting isProxiedOpenAI('openai') is true with baseUrl set, false without.

issue: No integration test exercises Brain against a real OpenAI-compatible proxy. Both the Gen 30 streaming bug AND this Responses API bug shipped because the test suite mocks the LLM layer entirely.
action: Add tests/brain-proxy.integration.test.ts: spin up a tiny node:http server responding canonically on /v1/chat/completions and 503 on /v1/responses (mimicking router.tangle.tools); construct Brain with provider=openai + custom baseUrl + model=gpt-5.4; call brain.complete; assert request hit /v1/chat/completions.
verification: Regression of finding #1 OR createForceNonStreamingFetch fails this test deterministically.

issue: assertApiKeyForModel(model) runs unconditionally even when --api-key is supplied; this aborted the validation harness on first invocation despite TANGLE_ROUTER_API_KEY being set, just under the wrong env name.
action: Skip the assertion (or pass the explicit --api-key into assertApiKeyForModel) when --api-key/--base-url are supplied via flags. Caller-supplied credentials should take precedence over env-var requirements.
verification: Invoke run-mode-baseline.mjs with --api-key + --base-url set and OPENAI_API_KEY unset; assertion should not fire.

issue: router.tangle.tools/v1/responses returns 503 'LiteLLM proxy not configured' which clients can't reliably feature-detect. A 501 Not Implemented with a specific message would let downstream clients fall back to /v1/chat/completions cleanly.
action: In tangle-router, either (a) add explicit /v1/responses proxy support via LiteLLM config OR (b) return 501 with 'endpoint /v1/responses not supported on this router; use /v1/chat/completions'.
verification: curl router.tangle.tools/v1/responses returns 501 with the documented body; bad CLI feature-detection (Finding #1's gate) would still work but routers that DO support responses can be detected.
